text.title: business-process-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for business-process-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Lifting APQC PCF text verbatim as if it describes the actual company processes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reference processes are industry averages; the company's reality may differ significantly. Every reference-derived sentence must be tagged [reference] and reviewed by a human before publication.

failure-mode.description: Documenting the ideal process instead of the actual process.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Analysis of an ideal state produces improvements to a process nobody runs; the current-state documentation must reflect observed reality, not policy intent.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Future-state benefits have no evidence anchor without volume, cycle time, and cost baselines; the business case is undefendable.

failure-mode.description: Cross-country process smoothing: treating a multi-jurisdiction process as one global flow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tax, payroll, and labor law vary significantly by country; a global process diagram that hides legal variants creates compliance risk and failed implementations.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enterprise programmes routinely "discover" 800+ processes; LLMs eagerly produce documentation for all of them. Without a Pareto cut, the artefact set is unmaintainable.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forcing every business onto APQC PCF or SCOR distorts genuine differentiators. Reference frameworks are scaffolding, not law.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A BPMN governance board produces standards nobody uses. The methodology cannot, by itself, prove adoption - instrumentation (audit trails, conformance checks) is required.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In M&A, the surviving entity's process is documented as canonical even when the acquired entity's is better. The methodology has no political guard-rails.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enterprise process landscapes drift quarterly (re-orgs, system changes, regulation). A 6-month-old BPA is already stale unless actively maintained.
